Product Name: Woven vinyl flooring delivered as tiles
Functional Unit: 1 m² of floor covering with a reference service life (RSL) of 1 year for specified characteristics application and use areas according to declared use classification ISO 10874.
Life Cycle Scope Description: Cradle to grave and module D (A + B + C + D)
Short Description: Bolon is a Swedish design company that makes innovative flooring solutions for public spaces. It is a third-generation family business run by sisters Annica and Marie Eklund. Under their leadership, Bolon has transformed from a traditional weaving mill into an international design brand with clients in different sectors all over the world. With a strong commitment to sustainability, Bolon designs and manufactures all its products at a facility in Ulricehamn in Sweden. The company is recognized worldwide for its award-winning flooring and its collaborations with some of the world's most acclaimed innovators and creatives.
Application: Suitable for hotels, shops, offices, and high traffic areas, such as public halls.
Material Composition Raw Text: Bolon flooring in tiles is composed by four layers, one weave and three backing layers, giving the product its unique qualities. The weave layer is made of vinyl warp and weft yarns tightly woven together. The variations of the warp and weft yarn together with the different weaving techniques gives Bolon flooring its vast design alternatives. Floors are graded into different classes according to their resistance to wear. For example, they are suitable for hotels, shops, offices, and high traffic areas, such as public halls
Recycled Content Data Text: Recycling is an integral part of our production, the recycled material is self-declared according to ISO 14021. Economic allocation has been used to distribute environmental burdens for recycled PVC and pre-consumer material.
End of Life Plan Data Value: At the end of its life the product is sent to either landfill or incineration with energy recovery.
Certifications List: EN 14041, CE-certified, M1, FloorScore, Green star Best environmental practice PVC
Long Description: All floor manufacturing takes place in Ulricehamn, Sweden. Here we manufacture the thread and the backing, weave the designer surface, and combine all these elements into a high-quality floor. Recycling is an integral part of our production, the recycled material is self-declared according to ISO 14021. The floor is then packed and shipped to customers. Installation is normally made with adhesives. Alternative installation methods are possible within Bolon recommendations. The floor coverings are water resistant and are cleaned using wet methods. Most cleaning needs can be accomplished with a vacuum cleaner, scrubbing brush, water, and a minimal dose of stain remover. At the end of its life the product is sent to either landfill or incineration with energy recovery.

Facility Address: The Watermill Wheatley Park, Mirfield WF14 8HE, United Kingdom
Functional Unit Text: The declared unit is one square meter of manufactured textile fabric and its packaging
LCA Table Context: LCA results for Craggan Flax are presented in Table 12 (environmental impact indicators, page 14-15) and Tables 14-15 (LCI resource use, waste, and outflow indicators, pages 17-19). The EPD scope is Cradle-to-Gate with End-of-Life, covering A1 (Upstream), A2-A3 (Core), and C1-C3 (Downstream). Stages A4, A5, and B1-B7 are excluded.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition for Craggan Flax is presented in Table 1 (page 4). Craggan Flax is composed of 85% Wool, 10% Flax, and 5% Polyamide, with an area density of 630 g/m².
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: Packaging (cardboard tube) contains 8.88x10-5 kg CO2e/m2 of biogenic carbon as noted in Table 4.
Fire Data Text: Deeply textured chunky weave fabric blending wool and flax. Color fastness to artificial light: 5. Color fastness to water - Wet: 3/4, Dry: 4.
End-of-Life and Disposal: US EPA data used: 66% landfilled, 19% incinerated, 15% recycled. Transport to disposal assumed 20 miles (~32km) by diesel truck.
Short Description: Craggan Flax is a warmly welcoming, deeply textured and thickly woven upholstery fabric made from 85% wool, 10% flax and 5% polyamide. It effortlessly blends comfort with style, bringing irresistible tactility to both task and soft seating. Certified with Indoor Advantage Gold.
Long Description: Craggan Flax is warmly welcoming, a textile to make any interior feel like home. Deeply textured and thickly woven, it effortlessly blends comfort with style to bring irresistible tactility and visual detail to both task and soft seating. Crafted from a blend of wool and flax, this sustainable fabric retains the raw appeal of its natural composition, whilst possessing the contemporary aesthetic of a chunky weave. Composed of 85% Wool, 10% Flax, and 5% Polyamide with an area density of 630 g/m² and a width of 140cm. Manufactured at Meltham Mills facility in the UK. The declared unit is one square meter of manufactured textile fabric and its packaging. The EPD scope is Cradle-to-Gate with End-of-Life per EN 15804:2012+A2:2019/AC:2021.

Functional Unit Text: The Declared Unit (DU) is one square metre (m²) coverage of substrate with one coat of paint. This is equivalent to 94.1 grams of Macphersons Durable Matt. The results are weighted averages of all shades and sizes of paint analysed.
LCA Table Context: The LCA results are presented for 'Macphersons Durable Matt (MD) All Shades', indicating that the product is part of a group. The results are weighted averages of various shades (902, 904, 906, Brilliant White, Magnolia) and pack sizes (2.5, 5, 10 litre containers) of paint, combined using production volumes. The life cycle stages included are A1–A3 (Product Stage), A4 (Transport stage), A5 (Installation stage), C1 (Dismantling), C2 (End-of-life transport), C3 (Waste Treatment), C4 (Disposal), and D (Benefits/loads beyond system boundary). The Use stage (B1–B7) modules are explicitly marked as 'MND' (Module Not Declared) across all tables, meaning they are excluded from the assessment. The LCA values are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, ADP-fossil) as rows and individual life cycle stages (A1, A2, A3, A1-3, A4, A5, C1, C2, C3, C4, D) as columns. Footnotes clarify that 'MND' means 'module not declared' and 'INA' means 'indicator not assessed'. Additionally, the mass of biogenic carbon containing material in both the product and its packaging is less than 5% of their respective total masses and is therefore omitted from this declaration.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for Macpherson Durable Acrylic Matt is presented in a product-specific table titled 'Main Product Contents'. The table lists key material inputs such as Solvent/Water, Pigment and Binder, and Additives, with their respective percentage ranges by weight. This structure clearly identifies the constituent materials and their proportions for the product.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: The mass of biogenic carbon containing material in the product is less than 5% of the mass of the product and is omitted from this declaration. The mass of biogenic carbon containing material in the packaging is less than 5% of the total mass of the packaging and is omitted from this declaration.
Density Text: The Declared Unit (DU) is one square metre (m²) coverage of substrate with one coat of paint. This is equivalent to 94.1 grams of Macphersons Durable Matt.
Short Description: Macpherson Durable Acrylic Matt is a hardwearing emulsion for interior walls and ceilings, specially formulated to allow scuffs and stains to be easily washed away. With built-in stain and moisture resistance, keeping walls looking cleaner for longer.
Long Description: Macpherson Durable Acrylic Matt is a hardwearing emulsion designed for interior walls and ceilings. It is specially formulated to be scuff and stain resistant, allowing for easy cleaning and maintaining a cleaner appearance for longer. The product's primary environmental impacts are linked to its raw materials, particularly titanium dioxide (pigment) and acrylic polymer (additive), which provide opacity. These materials are significant contributors to the paint's overall life cycle impact. The manufacturing process involves mixing these chemicals into a homogenous product, with minimal impacts during the application stage, where most impacts relate to application tools like drop sheets and rollers.

Product Name: Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat
Facility Address: Sculcoates Lane Hull HU5 1RU United Kingdom
Functional Unit Text: The Declared Unit (DU) is one square metre (m2) coverage of substrate with one coat of paint. This is equivalent to 87.2 grams of Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat. The results are weighted averages of all shades and sizes of paint analysed.
LCA Table Context:
The LCA results are presented for the product 'Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat' as part of a product group. Page 6 states that the products included in this EPD (902, 904, White, and Charcoal Grey shades in 1, 2.5, and 5 litre containers) were combined using production volumes to obtain a weighted average of impacts for all shades and pack sizes. Therefore, the LCA results represent this weighted average for the product group, not individual product variants.

The EPD declares that the following life cycle stages are included: Product stage (A1–A3), Construction process stage (A4–A5), End-of-life stage (C1–C4), and Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary (D).

Specifically, the tables (Pages 7-13) are structured with environmental indicators as rows and life cycle stages as columns. The columns represent:
- A1: Raw material supply
- A2: Transport
- A3: Manufacturing
- A1-3: Total of product stage
- A4: Transport to site
- A5: Construction – Installation
- B1-B7: Use stage (Use, Maintenance, Repair, Replacement, Refurbishment, Operational energy use, Operational water use) are explicitly marked as 'MND' (Module Not Declared), indicating they are excluded from the assessment.
- C1: Deconstruction, demolition (values are 0.00E+00, and Page 14 states it's considered immaterial).
- C2: End-of-life Transport
- C3: Waste processing
- C4: Disposal
- D: Reuse, Recovery and/or Recycling potential (Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries).

Footnotes indicate 'MND = module not declared' and 'INA = indicator not assessed', which apply to various indicators and the entire use stage (B1-B7).
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for 'Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat' is presented in a product-specific table on Page 4, titled 'Main Product Contents'. The table lists 'Material/Chemical Input' (Solvent/Water, Pigment and Binder, Additives) and their respective percentages by weight, shown as ranges (e.g., 25% - 29%). This structure clearly identifies the main components and their proportional contribution to the product's mass.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: The mass of biogenic carbon containing material in the product is less than 5% of the mass of the product and is omitted from this declaration.
Density Text: The Declared Unit (DU) is one square metre (m2) coverage of substrate with one coat of paint. This is equivalent to 87.2 grams of Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat.
Short Description: Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat is a water-based primer specially formulated for use with Crown Trade Fastflow top coats as a system. It is suitable for both interior and exterior applications.
Long Description: Crown Trade Fastflow Quick Dry Primer Undercoat is an advanced water-based primer and undercoat designed to be used as part of a system with Crown Trade Fastflow top coats. This product is suitable for both interior and exterior applications. The manufacturing process involves combining and mixing multiple chemicals and materials into a single homogenous product, which is then packaged and distributed. The primary environmental impacts are linked to raw materials like alkyd binder, titanium oxide, and carbon black pigments, which are crucial for opacity. The product's application stage has minimal environmental impact.

Functional Unit Text: All impacts are calculated using the declared unit “decoration of 1 m2 of surface with 1 layer of paint”
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for Dulux Trade Vinyl Matt are presented in tables across pages 6, 7, 8, and 9. These tables are structured with environmental impact indicators (e.g., GWP-total, AP, EP, PERE, PENRE, FW), output flows, and resource use as rows, and life cycle stages as columns. The assessed life cycle stages, as indicated by the EPD diagram on page 4, include A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport to customer), A5 (Application and use), C1 (De-construction demolition), C2 (Transport), C3 (Waste processing), C4 (Disposal), and D (Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries). Stages B1 through B7 (Use Stage) are explicitly marked as 'ND' (Not Declared) in the diagram and consistently show '0.00 E+00' in the LCA tables, indicating they are not assessed. Stage C1 also shows '0.00 E+00' in the tables despite being marked as 'X' (Assessed) in the diagram. A specific cut-off criterion is applied in stage A5, where energy consumed during application (e.g., for spray applications) is excluded due to its insignificance. Footnotes on pages 6 and 7 highlight that results for certain indicators (ADP-minerals&metals, ADP-fossil, WDP, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, SQP) should be used with care due to high uncertainties or limited experience, and clarify that the IRP indicator focuses on low-dose ionizing radiation, excluding nuclear accidents or occupational exposure.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition for Dulux Trade Vinyl Matt is presented in a table on page 3 under the heading "Component (> 1%)". The table lists key components such as Pigment (Lightfast pigments), Binder (Copolymer Dispersion), and Solvent (Water). However, the percentage by weight for each component is listed as "Confidential", meaning specific numerical values are not disclosed. The table structure clearly identifies the components and indicates that only those exceeding 1% are included.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: BBCpr = Biogenic carbon content in product. The value is 0.00 E+00 for all life cycle stages (A1-D).
Low Toxic Key Text: Our formulation is 99.9% VOC free, based on in-can VOC content and measured in accordance with ISO 11890-2:2013. It has also been independently tested for indoor emissions, including formaldehyde, TVOC, TSVOC and Cat 1A & 1B carcinogens. None of the substances contained in the product are listed in the “Candidate List of Substances of Very High Concern for authorisation”, or they do not exceed the threshold with the European Chemicals Agency.
Short Description: Dulux Trade Vinyl Matt is a 99.9% VOC-free emulsion paint, offering excellent opacity and coverage for interior walls and ceilings. Based on unique AkzoNobel technology, it provides a professional, high-quality finish with good flow and open time for easy application.
Long Description: Dulux Trade Vinyl Matt is a premium, high-opacity emulsion paint developed with AkzoNobel's unique technology. It delivers exceptional coverage and a professional, high-quality finish, making it ideal for interior wall and ceiling surfaces. The formulation is 99.9% VOC-free, as measured by ISO 11890-2:2013 in-can content, and has been independently tested for indoor emissions, including formaldehyde, TVOC, TSVOC, and Cat 1A & 1B carcinogens. It offers good flow and open time for easy application by brush, roller, or airless spray. This product is manufactured in the UK and is suitable for both commercial and residential interior applications.
Standard List: Product Environmental Footprint Category Rules - Decorative Paints version 1.0, 2018

Functional Unit Text: All impacts are calculated using the declared unit “decoration of 1 m² of surface”
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for 'Sikkens Exterior Solventbased Woodstain' are presented as a weighted average for a representative product, which is based on the annual production volumes and characteristics of 3 products within the Sikkens Exterior Solventbased Woodstain range. The EPD covers a 'Cradle-to-Gate with options' scope, including life cycle stages A1 (Raw materials supply), A2 (Transport of raw materials to manufacturer), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport to Regional Distribution Centre and customer), A5 (Application and use), C1 (Deconstruction), C2 (Transport to incineration or landfill), C3 (Waste processing), C4 (Disposal), and D (Benefits and Loads Beyond the System Boundaries). Stages B1-B7 (Use Stage) are explicitly marked as 'ND' (Not Declared). The LCA data is structured in tables where rows represent environmental indicators (e.g., GWP, AP, EP, FW, PERE, PENRE) and columns represent the different life cycle stages (A1, A2, A3, A1-A3, A4, A5, C1, C2, C3, C4, and B1-B7 as ND). The table structure directly aligns with the declared stage coverage, showing specific impact values for each assessed module. A significant footnote on page 9 explains that the Global Warming Potential (GWP) impact category has been modified to include VOC impacts, adding a generic factor of 4.23 kgCO2-eq/kg VOC, as CML-IA methods lack characterization factors for unspecified VOC emissions in GWP. This results in an additional GWP value ('GWP 100 years incl. VOC char. fact.') being reported separately.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for 'Sikkens Exterior Solventbased Woodstain' is presented in a product-specific table on page 3 under the heading 'COMPONENT (> 1%)* [kg / %]'. The table lists key components such as 'Pigment: Lightfast Pigments', 'Binder: Alkyd', and 'Solvent: Solvent'. However, the specific percentage values for these components are marked as 'Confidential'. A footnote indicates that these components represent '> 1% of total mass'. The table structure clearly identifies the material type but withholds quantitative data.
Low Toxic Key Text: Based on the recipe information obtained from the manufacturer, a few substances of very high concern for authorisation (in accordance with Article 59(10) of the REACH Regulation) where identified. All of the substances are present well below the communication and notification threshold of 0,1 % (w/w) as mandated in article 7 and 33 of the REACH regulation.
Short Description: This EPD covers a range of high-quality solventborne woodstain products for exterior use from Sikkens, designed for professional painters to provide long-lasting protection and finish.
Long Description: This EPD represents an average solventborne exterior woodstain product from Sikkens, intended for professional painters in the European market, Russia, and the UK. The product range includes Cetol Filter 7 Plus (satin, translucent, with UV absorbers for sun protection), Cetol HLS Plus (satin, non-film-forming), and Cetol Novatech (satin, translucent, film-forming, high solid). These are solvent-borne woodstain and wood protecting products for exterior use, formulated with alkyd resins to ensure excellent durability and finish while meeting environmental sustainability requirements. The LCA is based on production data from Montataire, France.

Product Name: Dulux Trade Weathershield Exterior Flexible Undercoat
Functional Unit Text: All impacts are calculated using the declared unit “decoration of 1 m² of surface”
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for Dulux Trade Weathershield Exterior Flexible Undercoat are presented as a single set of values, representing a group of 4 paint products within the same product line (Dulux Trade Weathershield Exterior Flexible Undercoat Medium Base, Deep Base, Dark Grey, Brilliant White). The LCA tables are structured with environmental indicators as rows and individual life cycle stages as columns. All valid life cycle stages, including A1, A2, A3, A1-A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, C2, C3, C4, and D, are explicitly assessed and included in the tables, as confirmed by the 'X' marks in the Modules Assessed table on page 4. Stages C1-C4 and D are included, with C2 covering transport to waste processing (incineration or landfill), C3 for waste processing, and C4 for disposal. For exterior paints, it is assumed that 88% of the sold paint goes to landfill and 12% to incineration. Footnotes on pages 6 and 7 indicate that results for ADP-minerals&metals, ADP-fossil, WDP, PM, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, and SQP should be used with care due to high uncertainties or limited experience with the indicators. Disclaimer [1] on page 7 clarifies the scope of the IRP indicator.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for Dulux Trade Weathershield Exterior Flexible Undercoat is presented in a table titled "COMPONENT >1% of total mass" on page 3. The table lists key components such as "Pigments", "Binder", and "Solvent". The values for these components are marked as "Confidential" in the "[kg / %]" column, indicating that the exact percentages are not disclosed, but the composition is product-specific.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text:
BCCpr = Biogenic carbon content in product
BCCpa = Biogenic carbon content in packaging
Low Toxic Key Text: None of the substances contained in the product are listed in the “Candidate List of Substances of Very High Concern for authorisation”, or they do not exceed the threshold with the European Chemicals Agency.
Short Description: Dulux Trade Weathershield Exterior Flexible Undercoat is a weather-resistant and flexible paint system. It provides longer-lasting protection for exterior woodwork and is suitable for wood, metal, and plastic surfaces. Applied by brush, it comes in 1L, 2.5L, and 5L pack sizes.
Long Description: Dulux Trade Weathershield Exterior Flexible Undercoat is a solvent-based paint, forming part of a 3-part weather-resistant and flexible paint system. It is specifically developed to offer extended protection for exterior woodwork, metal, and plastic surfaces. Application is recommended by brush only, avoiding temperatures below 5°C. The product is manufactured in Ashington, UK, and is available in 1L, 2.5L, and 5L capacities. Its production involves dispersing pigments in a binder and solvent mixture, followed by tinting, thinning, filtering, and packaging. The EPD covers a Cradle-to-Gate with options scope, including raw material supply, transport, manufacturing, application, and end-of-life stages.

Functional Unit Text: All impacts are calculated using the declared unit “decoration of 1 m² of surface”
LCA Table Context: The LCA results are presented for a group of four Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood paint products, including 'Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood Extra Deep Base', 'Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood Light Base', 'Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood Medium Base', and 'Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood Pure Brilliant White'. The data provided in the tables (pages 6-9) represents shared results for this product group, without differentiation between the individual variants. The EPD states that all major life cycle stages from A1 to D are included in the environmental performance assessment. The LCA tables are structured with environmental indicators as rows and life cycle stages as columns. The columns explicitly cover stages A1, A2, A3, A1–A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, C2, C3, C4, and D, aligning with the declared stage coverage. Stages C1–C4 (End of Life) and D (Benefits and Loads Beyond the System Boundaries) are assessed and included in the tables. Footnotes on pages 6 and 7 (Disclaimer [2]) indicate that results for certain environmental impact indicators should be used with care due to high uncertainties or limited experience. Disclaimer [1] on page 7 provides specific context for the IRP indicator.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood is presented in a product-specific table on page 3. The table lists components such as 'Pigments', 'Binder', and 'Water' with their respective percentages by mass, but the values are marked as 'Confidential'. This structure indicates that the composition is specific to the product, even if the exact quantities are not disclosed.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: BCCpr (Biogenic carbon content in product) is 0.00 kg C for all stages. BCCpa (Biogenic carbon content in packaging) is -3.56E-3 kg C for A1-A3 and 3.56E-3 kg C for A5.
Low Toxic Key Text: None of the substances contained in the product are listed in the “Candidate List of Substances of Very High Concern for authorisation”, or they do not exceed the threshold with the European Chemicals Agency.
Short Description: Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood is an advanced water-based, satin finish paint. It provides a smooth, durable top coat suitable for use on both interior wood and metal surfaces. It is designed for quick drying and easy application.
Long Description: Dulux Trade Quick Dry Satinwood is an advanced water-based, satin finish paint designed to provide a smooth, durable top coat for interior wood and metal surfaces. It can be applied using a brush, roller, or spray, with synthetic (nylon polyester) brushes recommended for optimal results with water-based paints. The product is available in 1L, 2.5L, and 5L pack sizes. The manufacturing process involves pre-weighing raw materials, dispersing pigments in a binder and solvent mixture, adding tinter for color correction, thinning to viscosity, filtering, and filling into appropriate packaging. The paint is produced in Machelen, Belgium, and distributed to customers in the UK. Its VOC content is 58.7 g/l.

Functional Unit Text: "The production and use of 1 square meter of 20 mm wood flooring for a lifespan of 50 years
LCA Table Context:
The EPD presents LCA results for three wooden floorings: DuoPlank® 20 mm, DuoPlank® 15 mm, and Lite 14mm. However, the document explicitly states on page 4 under "REPRESENTATIVENESS" that "DuoPlank® 20 mm is the reference product" and represents the "worst-case scenario" due to its highest material weight input. Therefore, the LCA data provided in the tables on pages 5, 6, and 7 are representative of the DuoPlank® 20 mm product.

The system boundaries for this EPD are defined as "Cradle-to-Grave and module D". The life cycle stages assessed are A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport of raw materials to manufacturer), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport to customer), A5 (Installation process), B1 (Use), B2 (Maintenance), C1 (Deconstruction and demolition), C2 (Transport of waste), C3 (Waste processing), C4 (Disposal), and D (Reuse, recovery, recycling potential). Modules B3 (Repair), B4 (Replacement), B5 (Refurbishment), B6 (Operational energy use), and B7 (Operational water use) are explicitly marked as "ND" (Not Declared) in the tables.

The LCA values are structured in tables with rows representing environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, ADP-fossil, PERE, FW) and columns representing the different life cycle stages (A1, A2, A3, A1-A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, C1, C2, C3, C4, D).
Specifically:
-   Product Stage (A1-A3): A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport of raw materials to manufacturer), and A3 (Manufacturing) are assessed individually and as a sum (A1-A3).
-   Construction Process Stage (A4-A5): A4 (Transport to customer) and A5 (Installation process) are assessed individually.
-   User Stage (B1-B7): B1 (Use) and B2 (Maintenance) are assessed individually. B3, B4, B5, B6, B7 are "ND" (Not Declared).
-   End of Life Stage (C1-C4): C1 (Deconstruction demolition), C2 (Transport), C3 (Waste processing), and C4 (Disposal) are assessed individually.
-   Benefits and Loads Beyond the System Boundaries (D): Module D is assessed.

Footnotes and assumptions impacting interpretation:
-   Disclaimer [2] on pages 5 and 6 states that "The results of this environmental impact indicator shall be used with care as the uncertainties on these results are high or as there is limited experience with the indicator." This applies to ADP-minerals&metals, ADP-fossil, WDP, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, and SQP.
-   Disclaimer [1] on page 6 for IRP clarifies that "This impact category deals mainly with the eventual impact of low dose ionizing radiation on human health of the nuclear fuel cycle. It does not consider effects due to possible nuclear accidents, occupational exposure nor due to radioactive waste."
-   For B1 (Use), "no impacts related to the use of the floorings are considered."
-   For C1 (Deconstruction and demolition), "The removal of the floorboards from the building is done manually, and therefore, no inputs or outputs are included in this stage."
-   For C4 (Disposal), the result was "adjusted based on the biogenic uptake in module A1" due to the release of biogenic carbon from incinerating the floorings.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for DuoPlank® 20 mm is presented on page 3 under "DETAILED PRODUCT DESCRIPTION" and in the "Product specification" table. The "Product specification" table provides product-specific details for DuoPlank® 20 mm, including its weight (13.4 kg), packaging weight (0.03 kg), product service life (50 years), area of application (Indoor), and geographic region produced (Netherlands). Below this, a "Component (> 1%)" table lists the main constituent materials: "6mm Oak or Ash top layer", "15mm Birch plywood", and "Glue (Polyvinylacetate)". The percentages for these components are marked as "Confidential". The table structure clearly identifies the product and its key physical attributes, along with its primary material components.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: BBCpr = Biogenic carbon content in product
Low Toxic Key Text: DECLARATION OF SVHC: None of the substances contained in the product are listed in the “Candidate List of Substances of Very High Concern for authorisation”, or they do not exceed the threshold with the European Chemicals Agency.
Density Text: The weight of one square meter of the product is 13,4 kg
End-of-Life and Disposal: The end-of-life stage is encompassed in these modules. The end-of-life scenario of the wooden planks is based on the latest version of the fixed values for end-of life processing scenarios, developed by Nationale Milieu Database (version 2024). Due to the release of biogenic carbon as a result of incinerating the floorings at the end-of-life, the result in moduel C4 was adjusted based on the biogenic uptake in module A1. For contaminated (coated, glued) wood EOL, 95% is incinerated and 5% is landfilled.
Short Description: DuoPlank® 20 mm is an engineered wood flooring for indoor use, featuring an oak or ash top layer and birch plywood. Produced in the Netherlands, it has a 20mm thickness, weighs 13.4 kg/m², and boasts a 50-year service life. It is sold globally and is designed for manual installation and removal.
Long Description: DuoPlank® 20 mm is an engineered wood flooring designed for indoor use, manufactured by Hakwood B.V. in Werkendam, the Netherlands. This product features a 6mm oak or ash top layer, supplemented with 15mm birch plywood, glue, coloring agents, and protective coatings. Each square meter of the product weighs 13.4 kg. It has a reference service life of 50 years, which includes maintenance such as sanding and painting every 10 years. The product is available in Europe, the US, and Asia. Both the installation and removal of the flooring are designed to be done manually, requiring no energy consumption for these stages. The EPD covers a comprehensive Cradle-to-Grave scope, including module D for reuse, recovery, and recycling potential, assessing environmental impacts from raw material extraction to final disposal.

Facility Address: Carnforth Business Park, Oakwood Way, Carnforth, Lancashire LA5 9FD
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for the product "OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED" are presented in dedicated tables. The scope of the EPD is "cradle to gate with modules A4, C1-C4 and module D". The modules A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport) and A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport from manufacturing site to central warehouse in UK), C1 (Deconstruction /Demolition), C2 (Waste Transport), C3 (Waste Processing), C4 (Waste Disposal) and D are included in the study. Modules A5 and B1-B7 are explicitly marked as "MND" (Module Not Declared). The LCA values are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., Acidification, Climate change – total, Abiotic depletion of fossil resources, Eutrophication, Water use) as rows and life cycle stages as columns. For "OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED", the following life cycle stages are represented in the columns: A1-A3, A4, A5 (MND), B1-B7 (MND), C1, C2, C3, C4, and D. C1 (Deconstruction/Demolition) is assumed to be manual, requiring no additional materials or energy inputs, hence values are 0.00E+00. C3 (Waste Processing) is assumed to require no significant processing prior to final disposal, with only minimal operations considered, hence values are 0.00E+00. Footnotes indicate that results for Abiotic depletion and Water use indicators and all optional indicators except Particulate matter and Ionizing radiation, human health, "shall be used with care as the uncertainties on these results are high or as there is limited experienced with the indicator." For Ionizing radiation, human health, the disclaimer states that it "deals mainly with the eventual impact of low dose ionizing radiation on human health of the nuclear fuel cycle. It does not consider effects due to possible nuclear accidents, occupational exposure nor due to radioactive waste disposal in underground facilities. Potential ionizing radiation from the soil, from radon and from some construction materials is also not measured by this indicator."
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for "OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED" is presented in a product-specific table titled "Product Raw Material Composition For Double Layered Parquet Flooring" (Table 1, Page 6). The table lists different product variants by ID and Name, and provides the amount of each material (Wood, Plywood, Oil, Lacquer, Adhesive) as a percentage by weight. For "OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED", the specific row (ID 3) details its composition.

Thermal Data Text: Thanks to its low thermal resistance and high dimensional stability, the product is particularly suitable for use with underfloor heating systems. Products are tested and classified with respect to key performance parameters such as dimensional stability, reaction to fire, release of formaldehyde, thermal conductivity, and durability.
Fire Data Text: Products are tested and classified with respect to key performance parameters such as dimensional stability, reaction to fire, release of formaldehyde, thermal conductivity, and durability.

End-of-Life and Disposal: At the end of its service life, the parquet flooring is assumed to be fully dismantled by hand (C1) and removed as separate waste. The collected waste is transported an average of 100 km to a waste treatment facility (C2). The entire product undergoes incineration (C4) with an overall efficiency of less than 60%. Module D accounts for potential environmental credits from energy recovery during the incineration of waste wood and packaging materials, replacing conventional energy generation.
Short Description: OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED is a double-layered engineered wood flooring from Havwoods' Italian Collection. It features an oak wear layer on a pine substrate, finished with multiple layers of UV-cured lacquer for high durability. Manufactured in Poland, it is suitable for residential and commercial interior use, including with underfloor heating systems. The product does not contain hazardous substances above 0.1% by mass.
Long Description: OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED is a premium double-layered engineered wood flooring product from Havwoods' Italian Collection. It consists of a hardwood oak wear layer, typically 3-6 mm thick, permanently bonded to a dimensionally stable pine substrate. The product is factory-finished with multi-stage lacquering processes, including primers, intermediate coats, and a UV-cured top layer, ensuring high surface durability, resistance to mechanical stress, and immediate readiness for use. It is available in semi-matt and extra-matt variants, often brushed and beveled on four sides. This flooring is suitable for interior use in residential, commercial, and public buildings, and its low thermal resistance makes it ideal for underfloor heating systems. The product contributes to indoor environmental quality and acts as a carbon store, and it does not contain REACH SVHC substances above 0.1% by mass.

Product Name: CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN
Functional Unit Text: DECLARED PRODUCT & FUNCTIONAL UNIT OR DECLARED UNIT: Interface modular carpet tile, one square meter floor covering. The functional unit is one square meter of floorcovering. MODULAR CARPET ON CQUESTTM BIO WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN VALUE UNIT Functional unit 1 m2 Mass* 4.03 kg
LCA Table Context:
The EPD provides LCA results for the product "CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN" across multiple tables, categorized by impact assessment methodology and region. The main LCA results are presented in:
- Table 6 (Page 10): North American Impact Assessment Results (TRACI 2.2)
- Table 7 (Page 12): EU Impact Assessment Results (EN15804+A2 (EF 3.1))
- Table 8 (Page 13): Rest of world Impact Assessment Result (CML2001 - Aug. 2016)
- Table 9 (Page 14): Additional environmental impact indicators from EF 3.1
- Table 10 (Page 14): Resource Use (ISO 21930)
- Table 11 (Page 15): Output Flows and Waste Categories (EN15804+A2 (EF 3.1))
- Table 12 (Page 15): Carbon Emissions and Removals (EN15804+A2 (EF 3.1))

These tables present environmental indicators (rows) across various life cycle stages (columns) for the declared product. The EPD scope is "Cradle-to-grave". The included life cycle stages are:
- A1-A3: Raw material supply, transport to factory, and manufacturing.
- A4: Transport from gate to site.
- A5: Assembly/Installation.
- B2: Maintenance (represented for one year).
- C2: Transport of waste to local disposal.
- C4: Disposal, with four scenarios: C4/1 (average EU EoL), C4/2 (100% landfill), C4/3 (100% incineration), and C4/4 (100% recycling).
- D: Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary (Reuse, Recovery, Recycling Potential).

The system boundary modules table (Table 5, Page 10) explicitly marks B1 (Use), B3 (Repair), B4 (Replacement), B5 (Refurbishment), B6 (Building Operational Energy Use During Product Use), B7 (Building Operational Water Use During Product Use), C1 (Deconstruction), and C3 (Waste processing) as "MND" (Module Not Declared). This indicates these stages are not assessed or excluded.

Additionally, the EPD provides GWP results for various yarn weights, which represent product variants:
- Table 13 (Page 16): Additional yarn weights A1-A3 TRACI GWP
- Table 14 (Page 17): Additional yarn weights A1-A3 TRACI GWP with product names
- Table 15 (Page 17-18): Additional yarn weights A1-A3 EN15804+A2 GWP
- Table 16 (Page 19): Additional yarn weights A1-A3 EN15804+A2 GWP with product names
These tables show A1-A3 GWP-total, GWP-fossil, GWP-biogenic, and GWP-LUC for different yarn weights. The product "CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN" is represented by the 20 oz / 678 g yarn weight in these tables, aligning with the A1-A3 values in the main impact assessment tables.

Footnotes indicate that C4/1* refers to the average EU EoL scenario as per table 2 of the UL PCR part A. Definitions for impact categories and resource use are provided below their respective tables.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data for CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE WITH SOLUTION-DYED PA6 YARN is presented in a product-specific table (Table 1.7. Material Composition) on page 5. The table lists components (Yarn, Primary Backing, Precoat Backing, Filler, Stabilization Layer, Secondary Backing, Antioxidant, Ethylene vinyl acetate, Pre-consumer recycled limestone) and their corresponding materials (Nylon, pre- & post-consumer recycled polyester, Vinyl acetate emulsion, Fiberglass mat, Bio-based filler) with their percentage mass. The values are shown as "% Mass*". The footnote "*Nominal values" indicates these are approximate percentages.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: CQuestTM Bio A non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resin, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, is net carbon negative. Interface’s CQuestTM Bio is a non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, which is net carbon negative. Table 12: Carbon Emissions and Removals includes BCRP = Biogenic Carbon Removal from Product and BCEP = Biogenic Carbon Emission from Product.
Low Toxic Key Text: VOC content is mentioned in Table 2 (Installation into the building (A5)) on page 8, but the value is "-".
Fire Data Text: Radiant panel (ASTM E-648) Class 1, Smoke density (ASTM E-662) < 450
End-of-Life and Disposal: At end of life, the product should be returned to Interface through Interface's ReEntry process. ReEntry allows us to reclaim the products we've created and use them as raw materials for new ones. The modular aspect of the product allows for easy reuse of the product. The product is intended to be reused or recycled through Interface’s ReEntry process.
Short Description: Interface CQuest™ Bio Modular Carpet Tile with Solution-Dyed PA6 Yarn is a commercial floor covering with a non-vinyl bio-composite backing that is net carbon negative. It is designed for modular installation and has a reference service life of 15 years. The product is manufactured in the Netherlands and Northern Ireland, with a focus on reuse and recycling through Interface's ReEntry program.
Long Description: Interface's CQuest™ Bio Modular Carpet Tile with Solution-Dyed PA6 Yarn is a commercial floor covering designed for modular installation in commercial buildings. It features a unique non-vinyl bio-composite backing made with bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, which is stated to be net carbon negative. The product utilizes solution-dyed PA6 (Nylon 6) yarn and a primary backing of pre- and post-consumer recycled polyester. Manufactured in Scherpenzeel, Netherlands, and Craigavon, Northern Ireland, it is committed to transparency and sustainability. The carpet tile has a reference service life of 15 years and is designed for reuse or recycling through Interface's ReEntry process at its end-of-life. Its modular design minimizes installation waste.

Product Name: SOUND CHOICE+ MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING LVT
Functional Unit Text: Interface modular resilient flooring, one square meter 4.5 mm Sound Choice+ LVT. The functional unit is one square meter of floorcovering. Declared unit: 1 m², Mass*: 6.32 kg/m².
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for SOUND CHOICE+ MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING LVT are presented across multiple tables (Tables 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12). The product does not have a dedicated LCA column; instead, environmental indicators are listed as rows, and life cycle stages are presented as columns. The EPD covers a 'cradle-to-gate with options' scope, with a warranted service life of 15 years, and maintenance (B2) represented for one year. The system boundaries include the following life cycle stages: A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport from gate to site), A5 (Assembly/Install), B2 (Maintenance), C2 (Transport), C4 (Disposal), and D (Reuse, Recovery, Recycling Potential). Stages B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, and C3 are explicitly marked as 'MND' (Module Not Declared) in Table 7. The tables provide impact assessment results using TRACI 2.1, CML 2001, and EN15804+A2 (EF 3.1) methodologies, as well as resource use and output flows. The EPD states that no known flows are deliberately excluded and no credits were taken for recycling of production waste. It also notes that EPDs do not indicate environmental or social performance benchmarks are met, and results rely on estimations, with comparability variations possible between different programs or LCA software/datasets.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition for SOUND CHOICE+ MODULAR RESILIENT FLOORING LVT is presented in a product-specific table titled '1.7. Material Composition' on page 5. This table lists various components such as PVC, Plasticizer, Filler, Glass fiber, Pigment, Misc., and Additive & Stabilizer, with their respective values expressed as percentages (%) by weight.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: Removals of biogenic carbon (excluding packaging) 0.001 kg CO2

Table 13. Fire performance characteristics for Sound Choice+
STANDARD SOUND CHOICE+
EN 13501-1:2018 Bfl – s1
EN ISO 9239-1:2010 (smoke development) 158.90%.min

End-of-Life and Disposal: The modular aspect of the product allows for easy reuse of the product. The product is intended to be recycled through Interface’s ReEntry process. At the end of life, the product should be returned to Interface through Interface’s ReEntry process by contacting Interface at +1 888-733-6873 for US. Contact +31 33 277 5812 for the Netherlands. Contact +44 (0) 800 313 4465 for UK. Or contact local customer service through interface.com. Disposal in municipal landfill or commercial incineration facilities is permissible in accordance with local regulations.
Short Description: Modular resilient flooring, 4.5 mm Luxury Vinyl Tile (LVT), manufactured by Interface, Inc. in South Korea. Designed for commercial applications, it has a reference service life of 15 years and is intended for recycling through Interface's ReEntry process.
Long Description: This Environmental Product Declaration covers all styles and patterns of Sound Choice+ modular resilient flooring LVT (luxury vinyl tile). The products are manufactured in Seoul, South Korea. The application of this product is intended for modular installation of resilient floor covering in commercial buildings. The product has a nominal thickness of 4.5 mm and a total weight of 6320 g/m². Its material composition includes PVC (27%), Plasticizer (9%), Filler (59%), Glass fiber (1%), Pigment (<1%), Misc. (2%), and Additive & Stabilizer (2%). The product is produced through hot mixing and a continuous lamination process. It has a reference service life of 15 years and is designed for reuse and recycling through Interface’s ReEntry process.

Facility Address: Crab Apple Way, Vale Park, Evesham, Worcestershire, WR11 1GP, United Kingdom
Functional Unit Text: The functional unit used in the study is defined as 1 m² of floor covering installed for use over a 1-year period. The corresponding reference flow for the product system is defined as the mass, in kg, of 1 m² of flooring product, excluding packaging and is presented in Table 9. For the present assessment, a reference service lifetime (RSL) is 1-year in conformance with the PCR.
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM) are presented in dedicated columns within tables that also include other products (Korlok Select/Korlok Reserve and Art Select Rigid Core). The tables are structured with environmental indicators as rows and life cycle stages as columns. The life cycle stages included in the EPD scope are A1-A3 (Raw material extraction and processing, Transport to manufacturer, Manufacturing), A4 (Transport), A5 (Construction - installation), B2 (Maintenance), C2 (Transport to waste processing), and C4 (Disposal). Modules B1, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, C3, and D are explicitly stated as not applicable or negligible. For KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM), the LCA results are specifically presented for "Distribution to the United Kingdom" and "Distribution to Australia" scenarios. The tables provide values for each of the included life cycle stages (A1-A3, A4, A5, B2, C2, C4) for each indicator. Footnotes indicate that results for some environmental impact indicators (ADPF, ADPE, WDP, PM, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, SQP) should be used with care due to high uncertainties or limited experience. Ionizing radiation potential (IRP) does not consider effects from nuclear accidents, occupational exposure, radioactive waste disposal in underground facilities, or natural radiation.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM) is presented in Table 7, titled "Material content for the flooring products in kg per square meter and percent of total mass." The data is product-specific, with a dedicated column for "Korlok/Van Gogh Rigid Core (6.5mm)". Values are shown both in kg/m² and as a percentage of total mass. The table structure clearly lists components (e.g., PVC, Re-grind, Filler) and their respective renewable and recycled content status.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: The flooring products do not contain bio-based materials.
Low Toxic Key Text: VOC emissions test method FloorScore®, Indoor Air Comfort Gold
Acoustic Data Text: a pre-attached acoustic backing specifically engineered to give excellent acoustic properties, reducing noise transfer to rooms below.
Fire Data Text: EN 13501-1:2018 - Fire classification of construction products and building elements. Classification using test data from reaction to fire tests. All Karndean Designflooring's LVT ranges achieve a reaction to fire classification of Bfl-s1.
End-of-Life and Disposal: At end-of-life, the products are disposed of in a landfill. The disposal stage includes demolition of the products (C1); transport of the flooring products to waste treatment facilities (C2); waste processing (C3); and associated emissions as the product degrades in a landfill (C4). For the luxury vinyl flooring products, no emissions are generated during demolition (C1) while no waste processing (C3) is required for landfill disposal. Transportation of waste materials at end-of-life (C2) assumes a 161 km average distance to disposal, as per PCR guidance. No recycling of the product materials is assumed at end-of-life.
Short Description: Karndean Designflooring's KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM) is an Engineered Plastic Composite (WPC/EPC) resilient flooring product. It is suitable for both commercial and residential interiors, available in planks, and features a rigid core with an acoustic backing. The product is designed for installation over uneven subfloors and aims to reduce noise transfer. It is disposed of in a landfill at end-of-life.
Long Description: Karndean Designflooring's KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM) is an Engineered Plastic Composite (WPC/EPC) flooring product, part of their Rigid Core range. It is suitable for both commercial and residential interiors and is available in various designs, formats, and sizes, including planks. The product is structured with multiple layers, featuring a rigid core for installation over uneven subfloors and a pre-attached acoustic backing for excellent acoustic properties, reducing noise transfer. Regrind material is incorporated into the stability layers. The primary materials include PVC, plasticizers, fillers, stabilizers, pigments, and coatings. Manufacturing occurs in China and Vietnam, involving mixing, extrusion, calendering into sheets, embossing, coating, and cutting into individual planks.

Product Name: Vinyl Based Wallcoverings on Non-Woven Backing
EPD Certification Body /Publisher/Progra: Institut Bauen und Umwelt e.V. (IBU) / UL Environment
Manufacturing Location: Multiple (DE, GB, BE, IT, US, FR)
Functional Unit Text: The production, transport and disposal of a weighted average of 1 m² vinyl wallcoverings on non-woven backing including packaging
LCA Table Context: The EPD presents LCA results for a weighted average of 1 m² vinyl based wallcoverings on non-woven backing, representing 15 participating IGI member companies. The system boundary is defined as 'Cradle to Gate with Options'. Life cycle stages assessed are A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport to building site), A5 (Assembly/Installation). End-of-life stages C2 (Transport), C3 (Waste processing), C4 (Disposal) are assessed. Module D (Reuse, recovery, recycling potential) is included. Use stage modules B1–B7 and C1 are declared as MND (Module Not Declared). Two impact methodologies are reported: CML 2001 – April 2013 and TRACI 2.1. LCA values are structured with rows representing environmental indicators (GWP, ODP, AP, EP, POCP, ADPE, ADPF) and resource use indicators (PERE, PERM, PERT, PENRE, PENRM, PENRT, SM, FW), and columns representing life cycle stages. Individual company GWP results may deviate up to 179% higher or 47% lower than the average.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition data is presented on page 3 under section 2.5 'Base materials / Ancillary materials'. A weighted average of the primary product components is provided as percentages: Non-Woven (paper 80%, PET 20%) at 35%, PVC plastisol at 32%, Inks at 14%, Chemicals and auxiliary materials at 11%, and Packaging at 8%. Pallets are considered part of the packaging. The composition excludes materials contributing less than 0.2% of total weight (cut-off criteria).
Low Toxic Key Text: It cannot be ruled out that individual wallcoverings may contain small amounts of substances that are included in the SVHC candidate list. This can be attributed, for example, to the contents of used waste paper. Depending on the manufacturer and wallcovering, different flame retardants, biocides and plasticizers can be used.
VOC emission content for this product: The wallcoverings meet the requirements of test standard ISO 16000 according to the French measurement method Arrêté du 19/04/11. Optional compliance with AgBB regulations also per ISO 16000.
Formaldehyde emission/content: Emissions of formaldehyde max. < 120 mg/kg according to EN 12149 - fulfills the norm
Fire Data Text: Fire performance according to EN 13501. Building material class B-D; Burning droplets s1-s3; Smoke gas development d0-d2. For USA: ASTM E 84.
Density Text: The grammage of the declared unit is 0.223 kg/m²
Locally Made Country: Multiple (DE, GB, BE, IT, US, FR)
End-of-Life and Disposal: Wallcoverings are subject to waste code 170904 (mixed construction and demolition waste) per the European Waste Catalogue. They can be disposed of as normal household waste (dustbin or refuse sacks). Used wallcoverings should not be placed in the waste paper bank. End-of-life scenario: 97% incineration, 3% landfilling. For LCA calculation, landfilling is chosen for the US and incineration for the EU.
Short Description: Vinyl based wallcoverings on non-woven backing are decorative interior wall coverings consisting of plasticized PVC on a cellulose/polyester non-woven substrate. Produced by 15 IGI member companies across Europe and the US, they weigh 0.223 kg/m² and have a 10-year reference service life.
Long Description: Vinyl based wallcoverings on non-woven backing, as defined in EN 15102, consist of plasticized PVC (vinyl) applied to a non-woven base substrate composed of a blend of cellulose (80%) and polyester fibres (20%). The product is available with relief or flat vinyl designs and is dimensionally stable to aqueous paste. Unlike conventional wallcovering, it does not require a soak time and can be stripped in its entirety (strippable as per EN 235) when replacement is needed. The declared unit is 1 m² with a grammage of 0.223 kg/m². The weighted average material composition is: Non-Woven 35%, PVC plastisol 32%, Inks 14%, Chemicals and auxiliary materials 11%, and Packaging 8%. It is used for decorative wall design in private or commercial interior spaces. The reference service life is 10 years per the German BNB system. Installation involves applying adhesive to the back of the wallcovering or the substrate using a brush or roller. The EPD is an industry average covering 15 of 67 IGI member companies across Germany, UK, Belgium, Italy, US, and France.

Product Name: Residential Broadloom Carpet with Polyester Face Fiber
Functional Unit Text: Functional Unit=1 m2 of flooring over RSL of 10 Years
LCA Table Context: The LCA results for 'Residential Broadloom Carpet with Polyester Face Fiber' are presented in tables on pages 12-14. These tables provide product-specific LCA results for a cradle-to-grave assessment. The life cycle stages included are: A1-A3 (product stage including raw material supply, transport, and manufacturing), A4 (transport to building site), A5 (installation), B1 (use), B2 (maintenance), C2 (transport to end-of-life), C3 (waste processing), and C4 (disposal). Modules B3-B7 (repair, replacement, refurbishment, operational energy use, operational water use) and C1 (deconstruction/demolition) have no environmental impact and are excluded from results. The assessment uses both CML 2001 and TRACI 2.1 impact assessment methodologies. Results are reported per 1 m² of flooring over the reference service life of 10 years.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for 'Residential Broadloom Carpet with Polyester Face Fiber' is presented on page 5 in Table 2 under section 1.8 'Material Composition'. The table details the component, material, and mass percentage for each layer of the carpet: Face Yarn (Polyester, 54.80%), Primary Backing (Polypropylene, 3.89%), Latex (SBR Latex, Calcium Carbonate, Sodium Polyacrylate, Ammonium Laurel Sulfate, 39.20%), Secondary Backing (Polypropylene, 1.84%), and Topical Layer (Hydrophobically Modified Thermoplastic Polyester, 0.22%). The face fiber is virgin Polyester produced internally at Shaw through polymerization and extrusion.
Low Toxic Key Text: Indoor emissions during the use stage have been evaluated via Indoor Air Quality testing (Green Label Plus). No health-related concerns are present during the normal use of the flooring. Module B1 includes indoor air quality impacts.
End-of-Life and Disposal: At the end of useful life, it is assumed 100% of the product is disposed of via landfill (2.38 kg), 0% is recycled, and 0% is incinerated, unless Shaw's re[TURN]™ Reclamation Program is utilized. Transport to end-of-life is 100 miles. Shaw has implemented a re[TURN]™ Reclamation Program for all carpet products.
Short Description: Residential broadloom carpet with virgin Polyester face fiber, featuring SBR latex precoat and woven polypropylene secondary backing. Designed for residential flooring applications with a 10-year reference service life.
Long Description: Residential Broadloom Carpet with Polyester Face Fiber is a tufted broadloom carpet designed for residential settings. The face fiber is made from virgin Polyester produced internally at Shaw through polymerization and extrusion, with face weights ranging from 10.5 to 90 oz per sq. yd., with a weighted average of 38.43 osy. The manufacturing process involves turning the Polyester into yarn through various processes depending on the desired look, then tufting the yarn into a polypropylene primary backing layer, applying an SBR latex precoat to ensure maximum tuft bind, and adding a woven polypropylene secondary backing layer. A topical layer of hydrophobically modified thermoplastic polyester is applied. The product has a total thickness of 9.14mm and product weight of 2.38 kg/m². It is manufactured in Dalton, GA in ISO 9001 & ISO 14001 certified facilities. The product meets Green Label Plus certification for indoor air quality and is rated Radiant Panel Class I for fire performance. Installation requires adhesive application, and maintenance includes regular vacuuming and annual hot water extraction. The reference service life is 10 years.

Product Name: Residential Broadloom Carpet with Nylon-6 Face Fiber
Functional Unit Text: Functional Unit=1 m2 of flooring over RSL of 10 Years

Modules B3-B7 (repair, replacement, refurbishment, operational energy use, operational water use) and C1 (deconstruction/demolition) have no environmental impact and are excluded from results. The assessment uses both CML 2001 and TRACI 2.1 impact assessment methodologies. Results are reported per 1 m² of flooring over the reference service life of 10 years.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition data for 'Residential Broadloom Carpet with Nylon-6 Face Fiber' is presented on page 5 in Table 2 under section 1.8 'Material Composition'. The table details the component, material, and mass percentage for each layer of the carpet: Face Yarn (Nylon-6, 43.70%), Primary Backing (Polypropylene, 4.83%), Latex (SBR Latex, Calcium Carbonate, Sodium Polyacrylate, Ammonium Laurel Sulfate, 48.70%), Secondary Backing (Polypropylene, 2.28%), and Topical Layer (Methacrylate Polymer, Hydrophobically Modified Thermoplastic Polyester, 0.55%). The face fiber is virgin Nylon-6 polymer.

End-of-Life and Disposal: At the end of useful life, it is assumed 100% of the product is disposed of via landfill (1.92 kg), 0% is recycled, and 0% is incinerated, unless Shaw's re[TURN]™ Reclamation Program is utilized. Transport to end-of-life is 100 miles. Shaw has implemented a re[TURN]™ Reclamation Program for all carpet products.
Short Description: Residential broadloom carpet with Nylon-6 virgin polymer face fiber, featuring SBR latex precoat and woven polypropylene secondary backing. Designed for residential flooring applications with a 10-year reference service life.
Long Description: Residential Broadloom Carpet with Nylon-6 Face Fiber is a tufted broadloom carpet designed for residential settings. The face fiber is made from virgin Nylon-6 polymer with face weights ranging from 11 to 100 oz per sq. yd., with a weighted average of 24.68 osy. The manufacturing process involves tufting the yarn into a polypropylene primary backing layer, applying an SBR latex precoat to ensure maximum tuft bind, and adding a woven polypropylene secondary backing layer. A topical layer of methacrylate polymer and hydrophobically modified thermoplastic polyester is applied. The product has a total thickness of 9.4mm and product weight of 1.92 kg/m². It is manufactured in Dalton, GA in ISO 9001 & ISO 14001 certified facilities. The product meets Green Label Plus certification for indoor air quality and is rated Radiant Panel Class I for fire performance. Installation requires adhesive application, and maintenance includes regular vacuuming and annual hot water extraction. The reference service life is 10 years.

Functional Unit Text: 1m2 fabric to be used as upholstery of furniture. The fabric weighs 0,290 kg/m2 and has a reference service life of 10 years. Reference unit: square meter (m2)
LCA Table Context: The product "Colour Connect" has its own dedicated LCA tables. These tables present environmental impact indicators and resource use indicators per square meter of fabric. The life cycle stages included in the assessment are A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport), A5 (Construction - Installation process), B1 (Use), B2 (Maintenance), B3 (Repair), C1 (De-construction / Demolition), C2 (Transport), C3 (Waste Processing), C4 (Disposal), and D (Benefits and loads beyond the product system boundaries). Modules B4 (Replacement), B5 (Refurbishment), B6 (Operational energy use), and B7 (Operational water use) are explicitly declared as "ND" (not declared). The LCA values are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, ADP-fossil, PERE, etc.) listed as rows, and life cycle stages (A1, A2, A3, A1-A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, B3, C1, C2, C3, C4, D) as columns, along with a "Total" column. This structure directly maps the declared stage coverage to the table columns. Footnotes and assumptions impacting interpretation include: Disclaimer 1 for "Potential Human exposure efficiency relative to U235 (IRP)" clarifies that it focuses on low-dose ionizing radiation from the nuclear fuel cycle, excluding nuclear accidents, occupational exposure, radioactive waste disposal, and natural radiation from soil/radon/materials. Disclaimer 2 applies to several indicators (ADP-minerals&metals, ADP-fossil, WDP, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, SQP), stating that their results should be used with care due to high uncertainties or limited experience with the indicator. Although module A5 is included, the EPD notes that specific construction/installation processes beyond packaging and construction waste are outside the scope of this LCA and should be added separately by users for their specific projects. This means the A5 values in the table are limited to packaging material waste and construction waste.
Material Composition Table Context: The material composition for Colour Connect is described in a narrative format rather than a table. It is explicitly stated that the product is "made from 100% flame-retardant post-consumer recycled polyester." This information is product-specific and indicates the composition by percentage weight.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: Biogenic carbon content in the product: 0 kg C. Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging: 0.4009 kg C. Uptake Biogenic Carbon dioxide: Packaging 1.47 kg CO2 (biogenic).

Fire class: EN 1021/1-2, BS 5867 crib 5, NF D 60-013, DIN 4102, Ubereinstimmung, UNI 9175 classe 1, Cal TB 117-2013, section 1, IMO 2010 FTP part 8, NFPA 260 class 1
End-of-Life and Disposal: Modules C3 (Waste Processing) and C4 (Disposal) are included in the LCA.
Short Description: Colour Connect is an upholstery fabric made from 100% flame-retardant post-consumer recycled polyester. It is designed for the international project market, including hospitality, healthcare, retail, offices, and education. It has a reference service life of 10 years and meets various fire resistance standards.
Long Description: Vescom's Colour Connect is a high-quality upholstery fabric specifically designed for the international project market, catering to sectors like hospitality, healthcare, retail, offices, and education. This fabric is crafted from 100% flame-retardant post-consumer recycled polyester, emphasizing its sustainable composition. It is intended for use as furniture upholstery and boasts a reference service life of 10 years, indicating its durability. The product undergoes rigorous testing for technical properties such as fire resistance, wear resistance, and tensile strength, complying with multiple international fire safety standards including EN 1021/1-2, BS 5867 crib 5, and IMO 2010 FTP part 8. The manufacturing process involves weaving colored yarn imported from Italy at the production site, followed by washing, manual inspection, packing, and shipping.

Functional Unit Text: The functional unit is 1 m2 of Wallcovering. The declaration covers Production (A1-A3) up to and including end of life phase (C1-D) of one square meter of vinyl wallcovering, including delivery (A4), mounting on the wall (A5), and maintenance during the product life cycle of 25 years (B1-B7). Emissions during the construction phase are not included. The reference unit is 1 m2.
LCA Table Context: The product OLEFIN WALLCOVERING 370 G/M2 has its own dedicated LCA results presented in tables. The tables are structured with environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, ADP-fossil, PERE, FW) in rows and life cycle stages (A1, A2, A3, A1-A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, B3, C1, C2, C3, C4, D, Total) in columns. The EPD states in section 1.8 that it is a 'Cradle to gate with options, modules C1-C4 and module D EPD'. The included life cycle stages, as explicitly marked with 'X' in section 1.8, are A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, B3, C1, C2, C3, C4, D. Modules B4, B5, B6, B7 are explicitly marked as 'ND' (not declared) in the scope table (1.8) and are not present as individual columns in the LCA results tables. The LCA tables (Pages 5-8) consistently present data for the product across these declared life cycle stages. Disclaimer 1 (Page 7) applies to 'Potential Human exposure efficiency relative to U235 (IRP)'. Disclaimer 2 (Page 7) applies to 'Abiotic depletion potential for non-fossil resources (ADP-minerals&metals)', 'Abiotic depletion potential for fossil resources (ADP-fossil)', 'Water (user) deprivation potential, deprivation-weighted water consumption (WDP)', 'Potential Comparative Toxic Unit for ecosystems (ETP-fw)', 'Potential Comparative Toxic Unit for humans (HTP-c)', 'Potential Comparative Toxic Unit for humans (HTP-nc)', and 'Potential Soil quality index (SQP)'.
Material Composition Table Context: Material composition for OLEFIN WALLCOVERING 370 G/M2 is presented in narrative form in section 2.1 PRODUCT DESCRIPTION. It describes the product as consisting of a TPO topcoat, printed with water-based inks, on a non-woven backing. The main component is TPO. No specific percentages by weight or mass for individual components are provided, except for the total weight per square meter.
Biogenic carbon content in Product Text: The following Information describes the biogenic carbon content in (the main parts of) the product at the factory gate per square meter: Biogenic carbon content in the product 0 kg C. Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging 0.009577 kg C. Uptake Biogenic Carbon dioxide Packaging 0.03512 kg CO2 (biogenic).
End-of-Life and Disposal: The EPD includes end-of-life phases C1 (De-construction / Demolition), C2 (Transport), C3 (Waste Processing), C4 (Disposal), and module D (Benefits and loads beyond the product system boundaries).
Short Description: Olefin Wallcovering 370 g/m2 is a decorative wallcovering made of a TPO topcoat printed with water-based inks on a non-woven backing. It is applied to interior walls using adhesive. The product has a declared life cycle of 25 years and is cradle-to-gate with options, including end-of-life and benefits beyond the system boundary.
Long Description: Vescom Thermoplastic Polyolefin wallcovering, weighing 370 g/m2, is a decorative interior wallcovering. It consists of a TPO topcoat, printed with water-based inks, laminated onto a non-woven backing. The wallcovering is supplied in rolls with a width of 130cm and is mounted on walls using Vescom adhesive. The production process involves printing the TPO foil, laminating it to the backing, embossing, cutting, and packing. The EPD covers a product life cycle of 25 years, including raw material supply, transport, manufacturing, installation, maintenance (B1-B3), and end-of-life phases (C1-D), with benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries.
